title: Jacob E. Gardner, M.D./M.P.H.
text: I am a General Surgery PGY-1 Resident at Montefiore Medical Center/Albert Einstein College of Medicine. My professional interests revolve around Surgical Oncology, Hepatopancreatobiliary Surgery, and Public/Global Health. Most of my research focuses on cancer epidemiology in developing countries. Test

title: Jacob E. Gardner, M.D./M.P.H.
text:
Work Experience
Montefiore Medical Center/Albert Einstein College of Medicine, Department of General Surgery | Categorical Resident | Jul. 2025 - Present
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Immunization and Respiratory Diseases | Research Intern | Oct. 2023 - Jun. 2024
Cleveland Clinic Foundation, Lerner Research Institute, Jung Laboratory | Clinical Research Coordinator | Apr. 2021 - Aug. 2025
Calmette Hospital, National Cancer Centre of Cambodia | Clinical Research Coordinator | Feb. 2018 - Aug. 2025
On this website, you will find a selection of my academic works, including peer-reviewed journal articles and poster presentations. You will also find news and updates on my current research projects, as well as information on upcoming events and conferences where I will be speaking. Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or would like to discuss potential research projects.

title: Projects
text:
arXiv Preprint | Code
Over the centuries, I have devoted countless hours to deciphering and translating the ancient scripts and dialects of the various peoples of Middle-earth. My goal is to gain a deeper understanding of the cultures and histories of these peoples by studying their languages. Currently, I am working on a monograph that explores the linguistic roots of the Elvish languages. Through extensive research and analysis, I hope to shed light on the connections between the different dialects of Elvish and their origins. This project has been particularly challenging, as Elvish is a complex and nuanced language, but I am determined to see it through to completion.

title: Projects
section: The History of the War of the Ring
text:
The History of the War of the Ring

arXiv Preprint | Code
I am creating a comprehensive and detailed history of the conflict that goes beyond the surface-level events. By gathering information from a variety of sources, including my own memories, written accounts, and oral histories, I hope to shed new light on this important period in Middle-earth’s history and provide valuable insights into the motivations and actions of the various players involved.

title: News & Updates
text:
I will be delivering the Keynote Address at the Cambodian Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons (CAOMS) & Dental Implant Society of Cambodia (DISC) 1st Joint Conference in Phnom Penh, Cambodia from June 13th to 14th 2024. The topic of my presentation will be “The Detection of Human Papillomavirus in Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma.”
